growth_records.cite: Tanouti et al. Structural insight into the novel Thermus thermophilus SPOUT methyltransferase RlmR catalysing Um2552 formation in the 23S rRNA A-loop: a case of convergent evolution. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf432. [PMC12123411]
growth_records.snippet: To determine whether growth is affected by the loss of RlmR, the growth rates of WT and mutant ΔrlmR T. thermophilus strains were compared. At 70°C, the doubling times for both strains were similar (WT: 71 ± 7 min; ΔrlmR
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (71 ± 7 min) explicitly reported in text for WT T. thermophilus grown in TSB at 70°C. doubling_time_h = 71/60 = 1.183 h. growth_rate_per_h (mu) calculated as ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/1.183 = 0.586 h-1. Oxygenation/aeration and pH not stated in text so left null; medium composition not detailed beyond 'TSB' (commercial formulation, not itemized in paper). [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Tanouti et al. Structural insight into the novel Thermus thermophilus SPOUT methyltransferase RlmR catalysing Um2552 formation in the 23S rRNA A-loop: a case of convergent evolution. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf432. [PMC12123411]
growth_records.snippet: To determine whether growth is affected by the loss of RlmR, the growth rates of WT and mutant ΔrlmR T. thermophilus strains were compared. At 70°C, the doubling times for both strains were similar (WT: 71 ± 7 min; ΔrlmR
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (68 ± 12 min) explicitly reported in text for ΔrlmR (ttc1712 deletion) T. thermophilus grown in TSB at 70°C. doubling_time_h = 68/60 = 1.133 h. growth_rate_per_h (mu) calculated as ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/1.133 = 0.612 h-1. Oxygenation/aeration and pH not stated in text so left null; medium composition not detailed beyond 'TSB' (commercial formulation, not itemized in paper). [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

up.method: HPLC residual-glucose time-course during exponential phase (used to constrain model)
Constraint value taken from previous publication [ref 39], not measured in this study

sec.method: HPLC (trace amounts during lag and exponential phase)
HPLC (trace; also used as measured exchange constraint per phase)

growth_records.cite: Na-Rae Lee et al. Genome-scale metabolic network reconstruction and in silico flux analysis of the thermophilic bacterium Thermus thermophilus HB27. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-13-61. [PMC4021367]
growth_records.snippet: The in silico predicted cell growth of 0.66 h-1 was very close (within the acceptable error range of 10%) to the experimentally observed specific growth rate of 0.64 h-1 ... in glucose minimal medium
growth_records.notes: Experimentally observed specific growth rate (exponential phase, glucose minimal medium) = 0.64 /h (in silico prediction 0.66/h). doubling_time = ln2/0.64 = 0.693/0.64 = 1.083 h (calculated). Glucose uptake was MEASURED during exponential phase and used to constrain the FBA model, but the numeric qGlc (mmol/gDCW/h) is not stated in the main text (in figure/Additional file), so left null. O2 uptake 10 mmol/gDCW/h is CITED from a previous publication (ref 39), NOT measured here -> is_experimental=false. GAM=58.34 mmol/gDCW/h borrowed from E. coli GSMM; NGAM=14 mmol/gDCW/h (vs 8 for E. coli). Acetate/lactate/ethanol detected in TRACE amounts by HPLC (no numeric rate in text). Medium = DMM + 0.6% (w/v) glucose = 6 g/L (~33.3 mM); full DMM salt recipe not enumerated so medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.medium: complex TM medium (supplemented with all 20 amino acids)

up.method: HPLC residual-substrate time-course; used to constrain model
HPLC; individual amino-acid consumption profiles measured and compared to biosynthetic demand

growth_records.cite: Na-Rae Lee et al. Genome-scale metabolic network reconstruction and in silico flux analysis of the thermophilic bacterium Thermus thermophilus HB27. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-13-61. [PMC4021367]
growth_records.snippet: the complex medium did not show any appreciable lag phase and the cells grew almost twice as fast as the minimal media
growth_records.notes: Growth rate NOT numerically stated for complex medium; text says cells grew 'almost twice as fast' as the glucose-minimal culture (0.64 /h). Estimated mu = ~2 x 0.64 = ~1.28 /h (calculated, approximate upper bound; true value likely slightly lower given 'almost'). doubling_time = ln2/1.28 = 0.54 h (calculated). Nutrient consumption/secretion profiles (glucose, trehalose, lactate, acetate, ethanol, 20 amino acids) were MEASURED by HPLC and used to constrain the model, but numeric per-phase rates not in main text. Complex TM medium supplemented with all 20 amino acids; composition not enumerated.
